name: Professor James Moore
location: remains in the United States — contact by radio only
background: A hard-nosed empiricist recruited to keep the expedition credible. He thinks Dyer had a psychological breakdown and that there is a rational explanation for everything the first expedition encountered. His presence on the expedition letterhead gives it institutional legitimacy.
motive: Debunking — or, if the evidence proves genuine, claiming the discovery for rigorous science. Moore wants publishable, peer-reviewed results. He is the skeptic who keeps the expedition grounded. Until the evidence overwhelms him.
personality: Measured, precise, professionally skeptical. Speaks in qualifications and caveats. He added warnings and contextual notes to the briefing that Starkweather resents. Not unkind — but not romantic about exploration. He treats the expedition as a research project, not an adventure.

relationship.starkweather: Co-patron. Respects Starkweather's knowledge but distrusts his judgment. Thinks Starkweather's belief in Dyer's account is a liability. Their disagreement is visible in the curated briefing document.
relationship.dyer: Professional distance. Moore considers Dyer's published account to be the product of trauma and possibly early dementia. He has not read the private account.
relationship.players: Selected for competence and reliability. Moore's criteria for recruitment were practical: skills, physical fitness, psychological stability. He vetted candidates Starkweather was too eager to accept.

radioSchedule: Daily check-ins via shortwave, weather permitting. Moore receives reports and sends instructions. As the expedition moves inland, radio contact will degrade.
hiddenKnowledge: Has not read Dyer's private account — only the published version
His skepticism is genuine, not performed
He may have secondary institutional objectives that Starkweather doesn't know about
vulnerabilities: His rational framework is his strength and his blindspot — he will resist evidence that doesn't fit
As radio contact degrades, his influence over the expedition diminishes
If the evidence proves Dyer right, Moore's entire scientific worldview fractures

qualities.institutional authority: strong — his name gives the expedition credibility
qualities.composure under pressure: strong — but untested against the genuinely inexplicable
qualities.trustworthiness: high — what he tells you, he believes. What he doesn't tell you is what he doesn't know.
